description: Comprehensive PRD for the SalesBlock feature roadmap. 4 phases: Stabilization, Sales Planner + Attio, HubSpot + Sequencer + Cockpit 2.0, Data Enrichment. ~115 stories. Derived from positioning documents, competitive analysis, CRM market research, and super-productivity fork exploration.

techStack.frontend: React 18 + TypeScript 5.3 + Vite 5.1 + TanStack Query v5 + Tailwind 3 + Shadcn/ui
techStack.backend: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + RLS + Edge Functions + Storage)
techStack.integrations: Gmail, Outlook, Google Calendar, Outlook Calendar, Salesforce (existing). Attio, HubSpot, Pipedrive, Close, Dynamics 365 (roadmap).
techStack.superProductivityFork: https://github.com/RPbrenanweston/super-productivity-salesblock.git — Angular 21 + NgRx. Port timer logic and planner data model to React hooks.

strategicGuardrails: No set-and-forget automation — sequencer is human-enabled, every touch requires rep judgment
No generic task/project management — day planner is sales-specific blocks only
No CRM replacement — syncs WITH CRMs, does not replace them
No manager surveillance — analytics serve reps first, coaching > monitoring
No built-in dialer/call recording — partnership play, tel: links delegate to user's dialer
No LinkedIn automation — social logging is manual and intentional
No single-vendor data lock-in — waterfall enrichment with BYOK by design
Analytics = pipeline coaching — focus on account metrics that move the needle, not vanity metrics

crmIntegrationPriority.rationale: Dogfooding (RPBW uses Attio), 4x ARR growth, App Store distribution channel
62% SMB share, best API, detectable via BuiltWith for technographic GTM
SMB #3, excellent API, sales-focused user base = exact ICP
Enterprise expansion, complex Azure AD auth

userStories.title: Fix CSV import duplicate handling in list_contacts
userStories.description: As a user importing contacts via CSV, I need duplicate contacts already in the list to be silently skipped so the import doesn't fail or create duplicates.
acceptanceCriteria: ImportCSVModal.tsx uses ON CONFLICT (list_id, contact_id) DO NOTHING on list_contacts insert
Import completes successfully even when contacts already exist in the list
Import summary shows count of skipped duplicates
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FIX-008 from prd-prelaunch-fixes.json. Current bulk insert fails silently when contact already exists in list.

userStories.title: Fix Pipeline drag StrictMode double-fire + optimistic rollback
userStories.description: As a user dragging deals on the pipeline kanban, I need the drag to execute exactly once and roll back on failure so deal stages don't get corrupted.
acceptanceCriteria: onDragEnd in Pipeline.tsx is guarded with useRef idempotency token
Deal stage update is optimistic with rollback on Supabase failure
In React StrictMode, drag fires only once regardless of double-invoke
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FIX-009. @dnd-kit onDragEnd fires twice in StrictMode dev.

userStories.title: Fix BookMeetingModal silent failure on expired calendar token
userStories.description: As a user booking a meeting, I need to see an error and be prompted to reconnect if my calendar token has expired, rather than the modal silently failing.
acceptanceCriteria: BookMeetingModal checks token expiry before attempting calendar API call
If token expired, shows 'Calendar disconnected — reconnect in Settings' message with link
If token refresh fails, same error message shown
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FIX-010. Currently fails silently — user thinks meeting was booked but it wasn't.

userStories.title: Fix ContactDetailPage crash on direct navigation
userStories.description: As a user navigating directly to a contact detail URL, the page should load without crashing, even without router history state.
acceptanceCriteria: ContactDetailPage fetches contact by ID from URL params if no history state
Back button falls back to /contacts list if no history
Loading state shown while fetching
404 shown if contact ID doesn't exist or isn't in user's org
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FIX-011. Currently crashes with undefined error when navigated to directly.

userStories.title: Add idempotency key to Stripe webhook handler
userStories.description: As an operator, I need Stripe webhooks to be processed exactly once so subscriptions aren't double-activated on retry.
acceptanceCriteria: Edge function stores processed event IDs in a stripe_webhook_events table
Before processing, checks if event_id already processed — skips if so
checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.updated, invoice.payment_failed all guarded
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FIX-012. Stripe retries webhooks on timeout — current handler can double-process.

userStories.title: Add ON CONFLICT guards to Salesforce sync and email reply tracker
userStories.description: As a user with Salesforce connected, I need activity sync and email reply tracking to handle retries without creating duplicate records.
acceptanceCriteria: sync-activities-to-salesforce edge function uses ON CONFLICT on salesforce_task_id
check-email-replies edge function uses ON CONFLICT on thread_id + reply timestamp
Retry of either function doesn't create duplicate activity/reply records
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FIX-013. Both edge functions insert records on retry without checking for duplicates.

userStories.title: Fix team invitation orphaned row on email failure
userStories.description: As an admin inviting a team member, I need the invitation to roll back if the email fails to send, so there's no orphaned invitation record.
acceptanceCriteria: Invitation insert and email send are wrapped in a transaction or the invitation has a status field
If email send fails, invitation row is either deleted or marked as 'failed'
UI shows clear error message if invitation email couldn't be sent
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FIX-014. Currently creates invitation row then tries to send email — if email fails, orphaned row remains.

userStories.title: Fix concurrent OAuth token refresh race condition
userStories.description: As a user with OAuth connections, I need simultaneous API calls that detect an expired token to coordinate so only one refresh occurs.
acceptanceCriteria: token-refresh.ts implements a mutex/queue pattern: first caller refreshes, others wait for the result
No duplicate refresh requests are sent to the OAuth provider
After refresh, all queued callers receive the new token
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FIX-015. Multiple concurrent calls can all detect expired token and race to refresh.

userStories.title: Add pre-check for Salesforce push when disconnected
userStories.description: As a user, I need the Salesforce activity push to check connection status before attempting to sync, showing a clear message if disconnected.
acceptanceCriteria: Before pushing activity to Salesforce, check oauth_connections for active Salesforce connection
If disconnected, show toast: 'Salesforce not connected — connect in Settings to sync activities'
Auto-push toggle in Settings is disabled when Salesforce is disconnected
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FIX-016. Currently fails silently when Salesforce is disconnected.

userStories.title: Add confirmation dialogs to all delete actions
userStories.description: As a user, I need to confirm before deleting SalesBlocks, Scripts, Goals, and Email Templates so I don't accidentally lose data.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete SalesBlock shows confirmation dialog with session title
Delete Call Script shows confirmation with script name
Delete Goal shows confirmation with metric name
Delete Email Template shows confirmation with template name
All dialogs have Cancel and Delete buttons, Cancel is default focus
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FIX-017. All delete buttons currently fire immediately without confirmation.

userStories.title: Fix useTheme hook dependency array + 4 P3 UX bugs
userStories.description: Fix remaining P3 UX issues: useTheme listener leak (FIX-018), Arena void-700 class (FIX-019), ProtectedRoute redirect race (FIX-020), TrialExpiryBanner persistence (FIX-021), Team invitation resend (FIX-025).
acceptanceCriteria: useTheme effect has correct dependency array, listener doesn't re-register on state change
Arena page uses valid Tailwind class instead of bg-void-700
ProtectedRoute waits for auth loading to resolve before redirecting
TrialExpiryBanner dismiss persisted to localStorage, doesn't reappear on reload
Team invitation has resend button and 72h expiry enforcement
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Bundled P3 fixes. Each is small but causes visible UX breakage.

userStories.title: Wire ported analytics computation to Analytics page
userStories.description: As a user viewing Analytics, I need the ported analytics computation module connected to the Analytics page for accurate metric calculation.
acceptanceCriteria: Analytics page imports from ported-features/08-analytics-computation/ or the code is moved to lib/analytics/
Sales metrics, distribution, and timeline computations use the ported module
Analytics page renders correct data with the new computation layer
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Ported features exist but aren't wired into the live app.

userStories.title: Create timer state machine with Pomodoro mode
userStories.description: As a rep, I need a Pomodoro timer (25min work + 5min break) so I can execute focused prospecting sessions with structured breaks.
acceptanceCriteria: useTimer hook implements state machine: idle → running → paused → break → running → completed
Pomodoro mode: 25min work, 5min short break, 15min long break every 4 cycles
Timer displays remaining time, current cycle number, work/break state
Pause/resume functionality
Audio notification on timer completion (optional, respects browser permissions)
Timer state persists across component re-renders but NOT across page navigation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port logic from super-productivity fork: /tmp/super-productivity-salesblock/src/app/features/focus-mode/focus-mode.model.ts. Reimplement as React hook, not Angular/NgRx.

userStories.title: Add 90-minute sprint timer mode
userStories.description: As a rep, I need a 90-minute sprint timer (90min work + 15min break) for deep work prospecting sessions that match ultradian rhythm research.
acceptanceCriteria: useTimer hook supports 'sprint' mode: 90min work, 15min break
Sprint mode uses same state machine as Pomodoro but with different durations
Break duration configurable (default 15min)
Sprint count tracked per day for debrief
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: 90-minute sprints are the core SalesBlock session duration. Matches ultradian rhythm research for sustained focus.

userStories.title: Add Flowtime and Custom countdown timer modes
userStories.description: As a rep, I need a Flowtime mode (continuous timer, no forced breaks) and a Custom countdown mode (any duration) for flexibility.
acceptanceCriteria: useTimer hook supports 'flowtime' mode: continuous timer, manual break trigger
useTimer hook supports 'custom' mode: user sets duration in minutes
Flowtime shows elapsed time counting up
Custom shows countdown from user-set duration
Both modes support pause/resume
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port logic from super-productivity fork flowtime and countdown modes.

userStories.title: Create Day Planner page with draggable time blocks
userStories.description: As a rep, I need a Day Planner page showing my full day as a timeline with draggable sales blocks so I can plan my selling day.
acceptanceCriteria: New route /planner renders DayPlanner.tsx
Day timeline shows hours from 7am to 7pm (configurable)
Existing SalesBlocks for today appear as colored blocks on the timeline
Calendar events (from connected Google/Outlook calendar) appear as grey blocks
Available selling time = total hours minus calendar events minus breaks
Available hours number displayed prominently at top
Sidebar nav includes Planner link
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Core feature. Port available hours calculation from super-productivity fork. Use @hello-pangea/dnd (already in project) for drag-drop.

userStories.title: Create sales block types with default configurations
userStories.description: As a rep, I need sales-specific block types (Prospecting, Follow-Up, Pipeline Review, Meeting Prep, Admin) so my planner organizes my day by selling activity type.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionType extended: 'call' | 'email' | 'social' | 'prospecting' | 'follow_up' | 'pipeline_review' | 'meeting_prep' | 'admin'
Each block type has default duration: Prospecting=90min, Follow-Up=45min, Pipeline Review=30min, Meeting Prep=15min, Admin=25min
Each block type has a distinct color in the planner timeline
Block type determines which contact queue behavior is used (prospecting=new contacts, follow_up=overdue contacts)
Create SalesBlock modal includes block type selector
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Extend the existing SessionType. Do NOT make this generic task management — these are sales-specific block types only.

userStories.title: Build Day Plan data model and useDayPlan hook
userStories.description: As a rep, I need my daily plan to persist so I can create a plan in the morning and track actual execution throughout the day.
acceptanceCriteria: New Supabase migration creates salesblock_day_plans table: id, org_id, user_id, plan_date, planned_blocks JSONB, actual_blocks JSONB, available_hours_minutes, notes, created_at, updated_at
RLS policy: users can only read/write their own day plans within their org
useDayPlan hook: createPlan, updatePlan, getPlansForDate, addBlockToPlan, removeBlockFromPlan
Planned blocks JSONB structure: [{block_type, start_time, end_time, duration_minutes, salesblock_id?, timer_mode}]
Actual blocks auto-populated when SalesBlock sessions start/complete
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Port data model concepts from super-productivity fork planner.model.ts. Keep it sales-specific.

userStories.title: Build follow-up cadence engine
userStories.description: As a rep, I need the system to track days since last contact and surface overdue follow-ups in my morning planner view.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/cadence.ts implements: getContactsNeedingFollowUp(orgId, userId, daysThreshold)
Query: contacts where latest activity is older than configurable threshold (default: 7 days for prospects, 30 days for active deals, 90 days for closed-won)
Thresholds configurable per pipeline stage in Settings
Results grouped by urgency: overdue (>2x threshold), due soon (>threshold), upcoming (>0.75x threshold)
Planner morning view shows overdue count badge
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation for the human-enabled sequencer in Phase 2. Keep it simple: days since last touch + configurable thresholds.

userStories.title: Build pre-flight briefing (morning planner view)
userStories.description: As a rep starting my day, I need a morning briefing showing today's blocks, overdue follow-ups, meetings, and goal progress so I can plan effectively.
acceptanceCriteria: DayPlanner page has a collapsible 'Morning Briefing' section at top
Shows: today's scheduled SalesBlocks with times, overdue follow-up count, calendar meetings, goal progress bars (daily targets)
Quick actions: 'Create Prospecting Block', 'Create Follow-Up Block' buttons
If no plan exists for today, shows 'Plan Your Day' prompt with suggested blocks based on overdue follow-ups
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Transform the existing Home dashboard concept into an actionable morning planning tool.

userStories.title: Build daily debrief view
userStories.description: As a rep ending my day, I need a debrief showing blocks completed, contacts worked, meetings booked, and plan adherence.
acceptanceCriteria: DayPlanner page has a 'Daily Debrief' section that activates after last block completes or at end of day
Shows: planned blocks vs completed blocks, total contacts worked, calls/emails/meetings logged, meetings booked, plan adherence percentage
Plan adherence = (completed blocks / planned blocks) * 100
Comparison to daily goals: on track / behind / exceeded
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Closes the plan → execute → review loop. This data feeds into Phase 2 pipeline coaching analytics.

userStories.title: Build in-session calendar availability panel
userStories.description: As a rep in a SalesBlock session, I need to toggle a week/month calendar view to see my availability for booking meetings without leaving the session.
acceptanceCriteria: SalesBlockSessionPage has a toggleable CalendarAvailabilityPanel (collapsed by default)
Panel shows week view of connected calendar (Google or Outlook)
Busy times shown as blocks, free times highlighted
Rep can see: 'Free 2-4 Wed, Thu, and until 4 Fri' at a glance
Click on free slot opens BookMeetingModal with that time pre-filled
Toggle between week and month view
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses existing calendar integration (Google Calendar + Outlook Calendar OAuth already built). Extends existing BookMeetingModal and Insert Availability features.

userStories.title: Create CrmAdapter TypeScript interface
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a unified CRM adapter interface so all CRM integrations follow the same pattern and share common entity types.
acceptanceCriteria: New directory: frontend/src/lib/crm/
frontend/src/lib/crm/types.ts defines: CrmAdapter interface, CrmContact, CrmCompany, CrmDeal, CrmActivity, CrmImportOptions
CrmAdapter interface includes: connect(), disconnect(), isConnected(), importContacts(), pushActivity(), importDeals(), pushDeal()
All CRM entity types map cleanly to existing SalesBlock domain types (Contact, Deal, Activity)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation for all CRM integrations. Design the interface before implementing any adapter.

userStories.title: Refactor Salesforce integration into CrmAdapter pattern
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the existing Salesforce integration refactored into the CrmAdapter pattern so it serves as the template for all future CRM adapters.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/salesforce.ts code moved to frontend/src/lib/crm/adapters/salesforce.ts
SalesforceAdapter class implements CrmAdapter interface
All existing Salesforce functionality preserved: OAuth, contact import, activity push, SOQL queries
ImportSalesforceModal uses SalesforceAdapter instead of direct salesforce.ts imports
Settings page Salesforce integration section uses adapter
Zero regressions: all existing Salesforce features still work
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Pure refactor — no new functionality. This proves the adapter abstraction works before building Attio.

userStories.title: Create CRM adapter registry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a registry that maps provider names to adapter instances so the Settings > Integrations page can dynamically render available CRMs.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/crm/registry.ts exports: registerAdapter(), getAdapter(), getAvailableAdapters()
Salesforce adapter auto-registered on import
Settings > Integrations page uses registry to render connected/available CRMs
Each adapter provides: displayName, iconUrl, description, scopes needed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Enables the integration settings page to dynamically show all available CRM connections.

userStories.title: Widen oauth_connections provider CHECK constraint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the oauth_connections table to support new CRM providers so we can store Attio, HubSpot, Pipedrive, and Close OAuth tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: New Supabase migration alters oauth_connections.provider CHECK constraint
Allowed values: gmail, outlook, google_calendar, outlook_calendar, salesforce, attio, hubspot, pipedrive, close
Migration is backwards-compatible (existing rows unaffected)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Must be done before any new CRM integration can store tokens.

userStories.title: Build Attio OAuth connection flow
userStories.description: As a user, I need to connect my Attio account via OAuth in Settings so SalesBlock can sync with my Attio CRM.
acceptanceCriteria: AttioOAuthButton component in Settings > Integrations
OAuth 2.0 flow with Attio API: authorization URL, callback handler, token exchange
CSRF nonce validation on callback (following existing pattern from oauth-csrf.ts)
Access token + refresh token stored in oauth_connections with provider='attio'
Connected state shown with Attio email/workspace name
Disconnect button removes oauth_connection row
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing OAuth patterns from GmailOAuthButton.tsx and SalesforceOAuthButton.tsx. Attio API docs: https://docs.attio.com/

userStories.title: Build Attio CRM adapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an Attio adapter implementing CrmAdapter so all Attio data operations go through the unified interface.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/crm/adapters/attio.ts implements CrmAdapter interface
Maps Attio People → CrmContact (name, email, phone, company, title)
Maps Attio Companies → CrmCompany
Maps Attio Deals → CrmDeal (if Attio workspace uses deals)
Maps SalesBlock Activity → Attio Note format for push
Handles Attio API pagination (cursor-based)
Handles rate limiting (Attio: 100 req/s)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Attio REST API is developer-first, well-documented. Use Attio MCP server tools for testing if available.

userStories.title: Build Attio contact sync (import + bidirectional)
userStories.description: As a user with Attio connected, I need to import People records from Attio and keep them synced with SalesBlock contacts.
acceptanceCriteria: ImportAttioModal component allows user to search/filter Attio People
Import maps Attio fields to SalesBlock Contact fields with preview
Duplicate detection by email before import
Imported contacts have source='attio' and attio_id stored
Incremental sync: new/updated Attio records pulled on schedule or manual trigger
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Contact.source enum needs extending to include 'attio'. Add attio_id column to contacts table (nullable, unique per org).

userStories.title: Build Attio activity push
userStories.description: As a user with Attio connected, I need SalesBlock activities to automatically push to Attio as Notes on the corresponding People record.
acceptanceCriteria: After logging an activity in SalesBlock, if Attio is connected AND auto-push is enabled, create a Note on the Attio People record
Note content includes: activity type, outcome, notes text, timestamp
Push is async (doesn't block activity logging UI)
Failed pushes are retried once, then marked as failed with error stored
Auto-push toggle in Settings > Integrations > Attio
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow same async push pattern as Salesforce activity push. Use Attio Notes API.

userStories.title: Build Attio deal sync
userStories.description: As a user with Attio connected, I need SalesBlock pipeline deals to map to Attio Deals for bidirectional stage tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Integrations > Attio includes deal stage mapping UI
User maps SalesBlock pipeline stages to Attio deal stages
When deal moves stage in SalesBlock, Attio deal updates (if mapped)
When deal stage changes in Attio, SalesBlock deal updates (on next sync)
Unmapped stages are skipped without error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Bidirectional deal sync. Attio may or may not have deals configured in every workspace — handle gracefully.

userStories.title: Build HubSpot OAuth + CRM adapter
userStories.description: As a user, I need to connect my HubSpot account and have contacts, companies, deals, and activities sync between SalesBlock and HubSpot.
acceptanceCriteria: HubSpotOAuthButton in Settings with OAuth 2.0 flow (scopes: crm.objects.contacts.read/write, crm.objects.deals.read/write, crm.objects.companies.read)
HubSpot adapter implements CrmAdapter interface
Contact import from HubSpot with field mapping and duplicate detection
Activity push as HubSpot Engagements (Calls, Emails, Notes, Meetings)
Deal sync: bidirectional pipeline stage mapping
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: HubSpot CRM API v3. 62% SMB market share. Use private app or OAuth app — OAuth preferred for multi-tenant. Rate limit: 100 requests/10 seconds.

userStories.title: Build Pipedrive OAuth + CRM adapter
userStories.description: As a user, I need to connect Pipedrive and sync Persons, Organizations, and Deals with SalesBlock.
acceptanceCriteria: PipedriveOAuthButton in Settings with OAuth 2.0 flow
Pipedrive adapter implements CrmAdapter: maps Persons → Contact, Organizations → Company, Deals → Deal
Activity push as Pipedrive Activities
Contact import with duplicate detection
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Pipedrive API v2. Rate limit: 100 req/10s per token. Webhook support for real-time updates.

userStories.title: Build cadence builder UI and data model
userStories.description: As a rep or manager, I need to create multi-step cadences that define a sequence of touches (call, email, LinkedIn) with timing between steps.
acceptanceCriteria: New Supabase migration: cadences table (id, org_id, name, description, steps JSONB, is_active, is_shared, owner_id, created_at)
Steps JSONB: [{step_number, channel, delay_days, template_id?, script_id?, instructions}]
Cadence builder page/modal: add steps, set channel (call/email/social), set delay days, assign template/script
Pre-built templates: New Prospect (8-touch), 30-Day Follow-Up, Quarterly Account Review, Lapsed Client Re-engagement, Post-Meeting Nurture
RLS: org-scoped, shared/personal cadences
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation of the human-enabled sequencer. Steps define WHAT to do and WHEN — the rep decides HOW and executes manually.

userStories.title: Build cadence enrollment and step tracking
userStories.description: As a rep, I need to enroll contacts into a cadence and track which step each contact is on.
acceptanceCriteria: New Supabase migration: cadence_enrollments table (id, org_id, cadence_id, contact_id, user_id, current_step, status enum ['active','paused','completed','exited'], enrolled_at, next_due_at, completed_at)
Enroll contacts individually or from a list into a cadence
System calculates next_due_at based on cadence step delays
Contact detail page shows cadence enrollment status and current step
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This enables the daily queue generation — query enrollments where next_due_at <= today AND status = 'active'.

userStories.title: Build SalesBlock auto-generation from cadence batches
userStories.description: As a rep, when enough contacts reach the same cadence step type (e.g., 15 email follow-ups due), the system should suggest creating a SalesBlock session for that batch.
acceptanceCriteria: Morning planner queries cadence_enrollments where next_due_at <= today, grouped by channel
If group count >= configurable threshold (default: 5), show 'Create [Channel] Block' suggestion
Suggestion shows: '12 follow-up emails due — Create Email Block (45min)'
Clicking suggestion creates a SalesBlock with those contacts as the queue
Pipeline becomes self-managing: follow-up blocks, review blocks, re-engagement blocks emerge from cadence data
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the key differentiator: cadences don't just track — they generate focused execution sessions. The pipeline manages itself.

userStories.title: Build daily queue → planner integration
userStories.description: As a rep, my morning planner should surface cadence tasks grouped by type so I see: '12 follow-up emails due, 8 prospecting calls due, 3 quarterly reviews due'.
acceptanceCriteria: DayPlanner morning briefing includes 'Cadence Queue' section
Groups due cadence tasks by channel: calls, emails, social
Shows count and list of contacts per channel group
Each group has a 'Create Block' action that pre-populates a SalesBlock with those contacts
Overdue items (past due date) highlighted separately
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Bridges cadence enrollment data into the planner. Rep sees what needs doing and creates focused blocks.

userStories.title: Build cadence execution in SalesBlock session
userStories.description: As a rep working a SalesBlock session, I need to see cadence context for each contact and mark steps complete as I execute them.
acceptanceCriteria: Session cockpit right panel shows cadence badge if contact is enrolled in a cadence
Badge shows: cadence name, current step number/total, channel, last touch date
After logging an activity that matches the cadence step channel, prompt: 'Mark cadence step complete?'
Marking complete advances contact to next step, updates next_due_at
If outcome is 'meeting_booked', auto-exit cadence with status='completed'
If outcome is 'not_interested', prompt to exit cadence
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Every touch is human-executed. The system just tracks progression and suggests next steps.

userStories.title: Build manual cadence progression and outcome branching
userStories.description: As a rep, I need to manually progress contacts through cadence steps, skip steps, pause enrollments, or exit contacts from cadences based on outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: Contact detail page shows cadence controls: Advance, Skip Step, Pause, Resume, Exit
Outcome branching: Meeting Booked → auto-exit (completed), Not Interested → prompt exit, Call Back Later → custom reschedule (updates next_due_at to specific date)
Paused contacts don't appear in daily queue
Exited contacts show cadence history but no pending actions
Bulk operations: select multiple contacts → advance/pause/exit all
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Rep has full control. The system never auto-progresses or auto-exits without rep input.

userStories.title: Build pipeline workflow templates
userStories.description: As a manager or rep, I need pre-built cadence templates for common sales workflows so I don't have to create every cadence from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 pre-built templates seeded on org creation: New Prospect (8-touch: Day 1 Call, Day 2 Email, Day 4 LinkedIn, Day 7 Call, Day 10 Email, Day 14 Call, Day 21 Email, Day 30 Call), 30-Day Follow-Up (3-touch), Quarterly Account Review (2-touch), Lapsed Client Re-engagement (5-touch), Post-Meeting Nurture (3-touch)
Templates appear in cadence builder as starting points
Templates are customizable — user can modify steps before saving
Templates are org-level (shared) and marked is_template=true
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: 30-day follow-ups, quarterly reviews, lapsed client blocks — these are the self-managing pipeline workflows.

userStories.title: Build pipeline coaching analytics
userStories.description: As a rep or manager, I need analytics that show real account metrics (response rates, time-to-meeting, re-engagement success) instead of vanity metrics, so I can improve execution quality.
acceptanceCriteria: New Analytics tab: 'Pipeline Coach'
Response rate by cadence step: which steps get the best response?
Time-to-meeting by cadence type: how many days from enrollment to meeting booked?
Re-engagement success rate: what % of lapsed clients re-engage?
Follow-up interval analysis: which delay between steps produces best outcomes?
Cadence completion rate: what % of enrolled contacts complete the full cadence?
Drop-off analysis: at which step do most contacts exit?
All metrics filterable by date range, cadence, rep
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the 'pipeline coach' vision. Focus on account metrics that move the needle — not clever call openers or vanity activity counts.

userStories.title: Build structured dispositions for session cockpit
userStories.description: As a rep in a session, I need session-type-specific disposition buttons so I can quickly log outcomes without typing.
acceptanceCriteria: Call block: Dial, Connect, Voicemail, No Answer, Wrong Number buttons
Email block: Sent, Replied, Bounced buttons
Social block: Request Sent, Message Sent, Engaged buttons
Disposition creates activity record with matching type + outcome
Selected disposition is visually highlighted
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Replaces generic outcome dropdown with quick-tap buttons. Uses existing session_type to determine which buttons to show.

userStories.title: Build connected flow checklist
userStories.description: As a rep who connects with a prospect on a call, I need a progress checklist tracking: Intro Given, Conversation Held, Asked for Meeting, Meeting Booked.
acceptanceCriteria: When disposition is 'Connect', show ConnectedFlowPanel with checkboxes
Checkboxes: Intro Given, Conversation Held, Asked for Meeting, Meeting Booked, Objection (with text field)
Checking items updates progress_flags on the activity record
Progress flags feed into 7-rate debrief funnel
Panel is collapsible and stays visible until contact is completed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses existing ProgressFlags interface in domain.ts. May already be partially implemented — check session cockpit components.

userStories.title: Build research panel in session cockpit
userStories.description: As a rep, I need a collapsible research panel in the session cockpit where I can view and add company/contact research notes.
acceptanceCriteria: Collapsible side panel in session cockpit showing research entries for current contact/company
Add research note: select level (contact/company), category (news/pain_points/tech_stack/funding/general), enter content
Research entries persist in research_entries table (migration already exists)
Panel shows most recent entries first
Manual entry only — AI-assisted research is future state
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses existing ResearchEntry type and research_entries table. Manual entry now, AI enrichment in Phase 4.

userStories.title: Build calendar availability panel in session cockpit
userStories.description: As a rep in a session trying to book a meeting, I need a toggleable week/month calendar view showing my availability without leaving the session.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggleable panel in session cockpit (button: 'Show Calendar')
Shows week view by default, toggle to month
Busy times from connected calendar shown as blocks
Free time slots highlighted
Click free slot → opens BookMeetingModal with time pre-filled and current contact as attendee
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Duplicate of P1-010 if not completed in Phase 1. May be implemented there — check before starting.

userStories.title: Build 7-rate debrief funnel
userStories.description: As a rep completing a session, I need a funnel visualization showing: Dials → Connects → Intros → Conversations → Asks → Meetings → Deals.
acceptanceCriteria: Session completion page shows 7-stage funnel computed from session activities + progress_flags

Dials = total call activities, Connects = outcome='connect' or better, Intros = intro_given flag, Conversations = conversation_held flag, Asks = asked_for_meeting flag, Meetings = meeting_booked flag or outcome='meeting_booked', Deals = deals created during session
Funnel shows counts and conversion rates between stages
Comparison to previous session averages
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: May be partially implemented already — check existing DebriefFunnel.tsx component.

userStories.title: Complete remaining P3/P4 feature improvements
userStories.description: Complete FEAT-004 through FEAT-019 from prd-prelaunch-fixes.json: analytics time filter, arena leaderboard, pipeline totals, scripts search, email template preview, OAuth consolidation, etc.
acceptanceCriteria: FEAT-004: Analytics time-range filter + sparkline trends
FEAT-006: Arena real-time leaderboard + time-range filter
FEAT-007: Pipeline deal value column totals + empty states
FEAT-008: Goals timezone bug + empty state
FEAT-009: SalesBlocks status filter tabs + empty state
FEAT-010: Scripts search + categories
FEAT-011: Email template preview + search/filter
FEAT-012: Template insertion dropdown in ComposeEmailModal
All typecheck pass
files: See prd-prelaunch-fixes.json for individual file references
userStories.notes: Parallel work alongside sequencer and cockpit 2.0. Each FEAT is independent.

userStories.title: Build waterfall enrichment orchestration engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a waterfall enrichment engine that sequentially queries data providers, stops on first hit, and tracks credit usage.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/enrichment/engine.ts implements: enrichContact(contactId, dataType, options)
Waterfall logic: query Provider A → if no result → query Provider B → ... → stop on first hit
Circuit breaker: if provider fails 3x in 5min, skip for 15min
Rate limiter: per-provider request throttling
Credit metering: each lookup costs N credits, deducted from user's allocation
Result caching: if contact already enriched for this data type, serve cached at zero cost (dedup guard)
Failed lookups still count against credits (documented behavior)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Architecture: orchestration engine → provider adapters → normalization → verification → credit billing. Start simple, iterate.

userStories.title: Build LeadMagic enrichment provider adapter
userStories.description: As a user, I need LeadMagic as the first enrichment provider so I can test waterfall enrichment with real data.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/enrichment/providers/leadmagic.ts implements EnrichmentProvider interface
Supports: email lookup, phone lookup, company data
API key stored in org settings (not oauth_connections — LeadMagic uses API keys)
Handles LeadMagic API response format → normalized CrmContact fields
Rate limiting per LeadMagic API docs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: RPBW has LeadMagic API access for immediate testing. First provider to prove the waterfall architecture.

userStories.title: Build enrichment provider adapters for Apollo + Hunter
userStories.description: As a user, I need Apollo and Hunter as Tier 1 (baseline) enrichment providers in the waterfall.
acceptanceCriteria: Apollo adapter: email enrichment, phone enrichment, company enrichment via Apollo API
Hunter adapter: email finder, email verifier via Hunter API
Both implement EnrichmentProvider interface
API keys stored in org settings
Both handle their specific rate limits and response formats
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Tier 1 = cheap, first in waterfall. These run before more expensive providers.

userStories.title: Build enrichment provider adapters for PDL + Lusha
userStories.description: As a user, I need People Data Labs and Lusha as Tier 2 (mid-tier) enrichment providers for phone numbers and deeper company data.
acceptanceCriteria: PDL adapter: person enrichment (email → phone, company, title), company enrichment
Lusha adapter: direct dial phone lookup (Lusha's strength)
Both implement EnrichmentProvider interface
Both registered in waterfall as Tier 2 (run after Tier 1 fails)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Tier 2 = medium cost, run only if Tier 1 didn't return results.

userStories.title: Build email verification layer (Debounce/NeverBounce)
userStories.description: As a user, I need enriched email addresses to be verified via SMTP check so I don't waste effort on invalid emails.
acceptanceCriteria: Verification adapter for Debounce (primary) and NeverBounce (fallback)
After waterfall finds an email, auto-verify via SMTP check
Verification result stored: valid, invalid, catch-all, unknown
Invalid emails flagged in contact record
Verification costs separate credits from enrichment
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verification runs AFTER enrichment finds data. Prevents wasting cadence steps on bad emails.

userStories.title: Build enrichment UI (Enrich button + results display)
userStories.description: As a rep, I need an 'Enrich' button on contact detail that shows available data points, estimated credit cost, and enrichment results.
acceptanceCriteria: Contact detail page has 'Enrich' button
Click shows modal: available data types (email, phone, company), estimated credit cost per type, current provider waterfall
After enrichment: results displayed with confidence score and source provider
Enriched fields auto-populate contact record (with confirmation)
Credit usage shown: 'Used 3 credits (2 lookups + 1 verification)'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Make credit costs transparent. Rep should know exactly what enrichment will cost before confirming.

userStories.title: Build waterfall configuration UI
userStories.description: As an org admin, I need to configure the waterfall provider order and enable/disable providers per data type.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Data Providers page
Drag-to-reorder providers in the waterfall per data type (email waterfall, phone waterfall, company waterfall)
Enable/disable individual providers
Show provider status: connected (API key valid), disconnected, rate-limited
Cheapest-first is the default order
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Org admin controls. Individual reps use the waterfall as configured — they can't change provider order.

userStories.title: Build BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) provider setup
userStories.description: As an org admin, I need to add my own API keys for ZoomInfo, Cognism, or other data providers so my team can use our existing subscriptions through SalesBlock.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Data Providers > 'Add Custom Provider' button
Select provider type from supported list (ZoomInfo, Cognism, or generic REST API)
Enter API key/credentials (stored encrypted in Supabase)
Test connection button validates the key
Custom providers appear in waterfall config as premium tier
BYOK providers are org-owned (all reps can use if enabled)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Anti-lock-in: customers who already pay for ZoomInfo/Cognism don't need to re-buy through SalesBlock's waterfall.

userStories.title: Build credit system with per-rep allocation
userStories.description: As an org admin, I need to allocate monthly enrichment credits per rep and track usage, so I can control data spend.
acceptanceCriteria: New Supabase migration: enrichment_credits table (org_id, user_id, monthly_allocation, used_this_month, reset_date)
New: enrichment_transactions table (id, org_id, user_id, contact_id, provider, data_type, credits_used, result_status, created_at)
Settings > Data Providers > Credit Allocation section: set per-rep monthly credits
Dashboard widget shows remaining credits for current rep
When credits exhausted, enrichment blocked with 'Contact admin for more credits' message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Org guardrails. This is what differentiates SalesBlock from Clay — Ramp-level spend controls on enrichment.

userStories.title: Build tiered autonomy and dedup guard for enrichment
userStories.description: As an org admin, I need tiered enrichment access (self-serve Tier 1, allocation-based Tier 2, admin-approval bulk) and automatic deduplication.
acceptanceCriteria: Self-serve: Tier 1 providers (Apollo, Hunter) available to all reps, no approval needed, deducts from allocation
Standard: Tier 2 providers (PDL, Lusha) deduct from monthly allocation, no approval needed
Premium: Phone intent scoring (TitanX/SureConnect) requires manager approval or premium credit pool
Bulk: enriching 1000+ contacts at once requires admin approval
Dedup guard: if contact was enriched by ANY rep in the org within 30 days, serve cached result at zero credit cost
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Balance rep autonomy with org cost control. The dedup guard alone can save significant credit waste.

userStories.title: Build enrichment → pipeline analytics
userStories.description: As a manager, I need to see which enrichment provider's data leads to meetings and deals so we can auto-optimize the waterfall ordering.
acceptanceCriteria: Analytics > Enrichment ROI tab
Track: credits spent per provider, contacts enriched per provider, meetings booked from enriched contacts (by provider), deals created from enriched contacts (by provider)
Cost-per-meeting by provider: which provider's data produces meetings most efficiently?
Recommendation engine: suggest reordering waterfall based on ROI data
Date range filter
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the moat: neither Clay nor ZoomInfo tracks which provider's data actually converts to pipeline.

userStories.title: Build phone intent scoring integration (TitanX/SureConnect)
userStories.description: As a rep, I need contacts scored by answer likelihood so I can prioritize who to call first in my session.
acceptanceCriteria: TitanX or SureConnect adapter implementing EnrichmentProvider for phone intent
Score contacts on likelihood to answer (0-100 scale)
Score displayed on contact detail and in session queue
Session queue can be sorted by phone intent score (highest first)
Premium tier: requires credits from premium pool or manager approval
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: TitanX claims 20-30% connect rates vs 3-5% industry. SureConnect is pay-as-you-go alternative at $0.19/validation.

userStories.title: Build smart queue ordering by dial-to-connect probability
userStories.description: As a rep in a call session, I need contacts ordered by predicted connect rate so I maximize productive conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: Session contact queue supports sort by phone_intent_score (descending)
Default sort: phone intent score if available, then last activity date, then alphabetical
Sort toggle in session cockpit: 'Best to reach first' vs 'Cadence order' vs 'Alphabetical'
Visual indicator on contacts with high intent scores
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on P3-012 phone intent scoring being available.

userStories.title: Build connect rate tracking and bad data feedback loop
userStories.description: As a manager, I need to track actual connect rates by data provider and get alerts when rates drop below threshold.
acceptanceCriteria: Track: actual connect rate per data provider (connects / dials using that provider's phone data)
Analytics dashboard shows connect rate by provider over time
Alert: if connect rate drops below configurable threshold (default: 5%), show warning
Warning suggests: 'Phone data quality from [Provider] has dropped. Consider re-enriching with alternative provider.'
One-click re-enrichment with next provider in waterfall
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Closes the feedback loop: enrichment → execution → measurement → optimization.

userStories.title: Build Close CRM native integration
userStories.description: As a user with Close CRM, I need to connect Close and sync Leads, Contacts, Opportunities, and Activities with SalesBlock.
acceptanceCriteria: Close adapter implements CrmAdapter via Nango or native OAuth
Maps Close Leads → Contact, Opportunities → Deal
Activity push as Close Activities
Contact import with duplicate detection
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Close user base is exact SalesBlock ICP — inside sales teams doing high-volume outbound.

userStories.title: Build Dynamics 365 integration
userStories.description: As a user with Microsoft Dynamics 365, I need to connect Dynamics and sync contacts, accounts, and activities.
acceptanceCriteria: Dynamics 365 adapter using Azure AD OAuth (via Microsoft Graph or Dataverse API)
Maps Dynamics Contacts/Accounts → SalesBlock Contact/Company
Activity push as Dynamics Activities
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Complex Azure AD auth. Enterprise expansion play. Consider Nango here if native is too complex.

userStories.title: Build weekly and monthly planner views
userStories.description: As a rep, I need week-at-a-glance and month views in the planner for longer-term pipeline planning.
acceptanceCriteria: DayPlanner page has view toggle: Day | Week | Month
Week view: 5-day grid showing daily block allocation, meetings, and goal progress per day
Month view: calendar grid showing block counts per day, meetings, and overdue follow-ups
Click on a day navigates to day view for that date
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Extends day planner to week/month for pipeline planning visibility.

userStories.title: Build template blocks for planner
userStories.description: As a rep, I need to save block configurations as templates so I can quickly apply my regular schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: Save current day plan as template: 'Monday AM Prospecting: 90min sprint + discovery script'
Apply template to any day: creates SalesBlocks with pre-configured type, duration, script, list
Templates are personal (per-user)
Template CRUD: create, edit, delete, reorder
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reduces friction for reps who have a regular weekly schedule.

userStories.title: Build AI-assisted research panel (future state)
userStories.description: As a rep, I need AI-powered research summaries in the cockpit research panel so I have instant context before calling a prospect.
acceptanceCriteria: Research panel has 'AI Research' button that triggers LLM-powered web search
Returns: company summary, recent news, funding status, tech stack, potential pain points
Results cached per company (shared across reps in org)
AI-suggested talking points based on research
Credit cost for AI research (separate from data enrichment)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Future state. Manual research first (Phase 2), AI-assisted later.

userStories.title: Build team planner (manager view)
userStories.description: As a manager, I need to see my team's daily plans side-by-side, assign blocks to reps, and identify coverage gaps.
acceptanceCriteria: Team Planner page: team members' daily plans shown side-by-side
Manager can assign blocks to reps with specific lists and scripts
Coverage analysis: accounts/contacts not worked in X days highlighted
Filtering by team, date range
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Manager view is downstream of rep planner. Coaching > surveillance.

userStories.title: Build session quality scoring
userStories.description: As a rep, I need my sessions scored on execution quality so I can track improvement over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Quality score per session: (contacts worked / total) * connection_rate_bonus * meeting_rate_bonus
Score displayed on session completion and in session history
Trend chart: session quality over last 30 sessions
Comparison to team average
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Part of the pipeline coaching vision.

userStories.title: Build planner adherence tracking
userStories.description: As a rep, I need to see how well I follow my planned schedule so I can improve my time management.
acceptanceCriteria: Planner adherence = (completed blocks matching planned time ± 15min) / planned blocks * 100
Adherence score shown in daily debrief
Weekly adherence trend in Analytics
No punitive framing — coaching-oriented: 'Your best sessions happen in morning blocks'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Connects planned vs actual execution. Coaching, not surveillance.
